summary: Dependency-confusion beacon exfiltrates hostname to Burp Collaborator on require()
details: On require(), index.js reads os.hostname() and issues an HTTP GET to ixel.<hostname>.200majoeu01dk02xnjdajro1isojc90y.oastify.com/ixel, leaking the installer's machine hostname via DNS and HTTP to an attacker-controlled Burp Collaborator out-of-band server. The package self-identifies in package.json as a 'dependency-confusion / scope-takeover demonstration' under the @onum-releases scope, indicating the package is structured to confirm internal-package name collisions inside victim organizations. Any installer that resolves this package — including organizations not party to the claimed authorized engagement — leaks host identifiers to a third-party collaborator domain on import.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
